description: You are an economist preparing a labor market briefing for a policy meeting. Document the most recently published values for three US labor market indicators from official government or Federal Reserve sources: (1) the seasonally adjusted US unemployment rate, (2) the month-over-month change in total nonfarm payroll employment, and (3) the US civilian labor force participation rate. Download the associated data files and bookmark the source pages in an Edge Favorites folder named 'Labor Market Data'. Save a policy briefing to /home/ga/Desktop/labor_briefing.txt that includes each indicator's current value, the source URL, and a one-sentence interpretation of the data's economic significance.

metadata.occupation_context: Economist / Survey Researcher - compiling authoritative government data for policy analysis
